commands.review_summary:
The branch updates ClawDock helper compose arguments and README documentation so `docker-compose.override.yml` is included before `docker-compose.extra.yml`, with a new regression test for helper argument construction.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main passes explicit `-f` arguments without adding `docker-compose.override.yml`, while Docker Compose merges only the command-line file list in order. I did not run a live Docker Compose repro in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(clawdock): load docker-compose.override.yml
target.body:
Fixes #49909.

## Summary
- Load `docker-compose.override.yml` when ClawDock helpers invoke Docker Compose with explicit `-f` args.
- Keep `docker-compose.extra.yml` loaded after the standard override and document the order.
- Add a regression test for `_clawdock_compose` argument construction.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/clawdock-helpers.test.ts`
- `bash -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh`
- `zsh -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: ClawDock commands now include `docker-compose.override.yml` when present instead of silently ignoring it after explicit `-f` args disable Compose's default auto-merge.
Real environment tested: Local macOS shell with a stubbed `docker` command and temporary ClawDock project files.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/clawdock-helpers.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: The regression test records Docker Compose args as base compose file, standard override, extra override, then the requested command.
Observed result after fix: 1 Vitest file passed, 1 test passed.
What was not tested: Live Docker Compose startup against a real OpenClaw container.
